market_note: Six federal agencies (OCC, FDIC, NCUA, Treasury, FinCEN, OFAC) missed the July 18 statutory deadline to finalize GENIUS Act stablecoin rules, leaving reserve and AML standards unsettled a year after passage.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act rulemaking deadline missed
stablecoin_items.summary: As of July 19, none of the six federal regulators had finalized implementing rules for the GENIUS Act, one year after it was signed into law.

stablecoin_items.headline: Circle wins OCC trust bank charter
stablecoin_items.summary: The OCC approved Circle to operate as a national trust bank, letting it manage USDC reserves (over $73B in circulation) directly; shares jumped on the news.

stablecoin_items.headline: Visa launches stablecoin platform for banks
stablecoin_items.summary: Visa unveiled the Visa Stablecoin Platform, letting financial firms issue and manage stablecoins; it will initially back Open USD, a new coin from a 140+ member consortium including Stripe and Mastercard.

stablecoin_items.headline: Stablecoin volume tops $7.5T, passes ACH
stablecoin_items.summary: On-chain stablecoin settlement volume climbed past the U.S. ACH network, with market cap now around $316.7B, underscoring stablecoins' shift into mainstream payment rails.

hack_items.headline: Ostium hit by $18M oracle exploit
hack_items.summary: An attacker abused a registered price-feed forwarder on the Arbitrum-based RWA perps exchange Ostium to fake trade profits, draining about $18M in USDC on July 15.

hack_items.headline: Across Protocol suffers first Solana-side attack
hack_items.summary: Across paused and then restored Solana deposits after its first-ever attack on that chain; no user funds were lost, with relayer losses absorbed by Risk Labs.

hack_items.headline: Cascade and DeFiTuna add to $20M weekly loss tally
hack_items.summary: A locked-fund drain at Cascade and a Solana lending shortfall at DeFiTuna pushed the week's confirmed DeFi damage above $20M across four separate incidents.

hack_items.headline: Ethical hackers flag $70B Aptos risk
hack_items.summary: Security firm Hexens disclosed a now-patched Aptos consensus flaw that could have been exploited for under $1,000 with a near-90% success rate, putting billions in assets and bridges at theoretical risk.
